Author: Dr Derek Austin
Title: Coding with LLMs Is Far From the Best Use Case of LLMs for SWEs
Text:
Member-only story
Coding with LLMs Is Far From the Best Use Case of LLMs for SWEs
Lead SWEs like you and me aren’t going to get great results from gaslighting LLMs trying to “one-shot” our engineering problems. But code is far from the best use case of LLMs for SWEs. Here’s why.
Do you agree with the quote (from Hacker News user lubujackson) that “LLMs are more deterministic than they seem?”
“[C]oding with LLMs is really just a higher level abstraction. And the good news of that is LLMs are more deterministic than they seem, which is a lot of what people are fearful of losing by giving LLMs ‘the reins’.” — lubujackson on HN
I agree with them myself, because LLMs are trained heavily to follow instructions. In my mind, that makes them not particularly good coders.
We SWEs understand that solving engineering problems involves a lot of creative problem solving, not necessarily “just following instructions.”
If coding was just following instructions step-by-step all day, then anybody could do it, with no training. And, if that were true, PMs would code all day instead of complaining all the time about how they’re “not technical.”
